title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 1
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: Your college principal has asked you to write a report on the mental health support currently available to students at your institution. Your report should assess what provision exists, identify gaps in the current provision, and make recommendations for improvement.
tasks.modelAnswer:
MENTAL HEALTH SUPPORT FOR STUDENTS: CURRENT PROVISION AND RECOMMENDATIONS

Introduction
This report assesses the mental health support available to students at Westbridge College, identifies shortcomings in the current provision, and proposes concrete improvements.

Current Provision
The college currently offers a drop-in counselling service staffed by two part-time counsellors, available on Tuesday and Thursday afternoons. An online resource hub provides self-help materials on stress management and exam anxiety. Additionally, personal tutors are encouraged to monitor student welfare during scheduled meetings.

Identified Gaps
Despite these provisions, several significant gaps exist. The counselling service is severely under-resourced; students frequently report waiting two to three weeks for an appointment. Evening and weekend support is entirely absent, leaving students isolated during high-stress periods such as assessments. Furthermore, personal tutors lack formal mental health training, limiting their effectiveness in identifying and responding to student distress.

Recommendations
Firstly, the college should recruit at least two additional full-time counsellors to reduce waiting times and extend service hours. Secondly, an anonymous online referral system would allow students to seek support discreetly, reducing the barrier of stigma. Thirdly, mandatory mental health first aid training should be introduced for all academic and pastoral staff.

Conclusion
With targeted investment and structural change, the college can create an environment where student mental health is genuinely prioritised. Implementing these recommendations would yield measurable improvements in student wellbeing and academic performance.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt: You have read an article in a national newspaper arguing that mental health problems among young people are exaggerated by the media. Write a letter to the editor responding to this article. In your letter, express your views, provide evidence to counter the claim, and suggest what media coverage should focus on instead.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Editor,

I am writing in response to the article published in last Tuesday's edition, which claimed that mental health difficulties among young people are largely a media fabrication. I feel compelled to challenge this assertion, which I consider both misleading and potentially harmful.

The suggestion that youth mental health concerns are exaggerated is contradicted by substantial evidence. The World Health Organization reports that depression is the leading cause of disability among adolescents globally, while domestic research consistently shows rising rates of anxiety, self-harm, and eating disorders in this age group. These are not media-manufactured trends; they are clinical realities experienced by millions of young people.

Furthermore, dismissing these struggles in a prominent publication risks reinforcing exactly the kind of stigma that discourages young people from seeking help. When trusted sources imply that mental illness is overstated, it sends a damaging message that those who suffer are being dramatic or dishonest. The consequences of such attitudes can be severe.

Rather than scepticism, I would urge your publication to redirect its coverage towards constructive narratives: profiling effective therapeutic interventions, celebrating schools that have embedded wellbeing into their curricula, and highlighting the work of charities providing frontline support. Responsible journalism on this subject has the power to reduce stigma and connect vulnerable readers with vital resources.

I trust you will reflect carefully on the impact of the positions your newspaper chooses to platform.

Yours faithfully,
A Concerned Reader

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: A mental health awareness website has asked readers to submit reviews of books, films, or podcasts that have helped them understand mental health issues better. Write a review of a book, film, or podcast you have encountered, explaining what it covers, what you found most valuable, and whether you would recommend it.
tasks.modelAnswer:
FINDING LIGHT: A REVIEW OF 'THE MINDFULNESS PRESCRIPTION'

'The Mindfulness Prescription' is a non-fiction podcast series hosted by clinical psychologist Dr Sarah Hollis, comprising twelve episodes that explore evidence-based approaches to managing anxiety, depression, and stress. It is aimed at a general adult audience and requires no prior knowledge of psychology.

What distinguishes this series from the saturated wellness market is its rigorous grounding in research. Each episode pairs accessible explanation with genuine scientific evidence, avoiding the vague platitudes that so often characterise popular mental health content. Episodes on cognitive behavioural therapy and acceptance and commitment therapy are particularly illuminating, presenting complex therapeutic frameworks in terms that feel immediately applicable to everyday life.

The most valuable aspect, however, is Dr Hollis's consistent emphasis on normalising help-seeking behaviour. Through candid interviews with people who have experienced mental health difficulties, the podcast dismantles the notion that struggling is shameful. Listeners are reminded repeatedly that professional support is both available and effective, a message that feels genuinely impactful.

If anything, some episodes could benefit from greater diversity in the voices featured, as the majority of interviewees share similar demographic backgrounds. Nevertheless, this is a minor criticism of an otherwise outstanding resource.

For anyone seeking to develop a deeper, more informed understanding of mental health — whether for personal reasons or out of professional curiosity — 'The Mindfulness Prescription' comes highly recommended. It is one of the rare wellness resources that truly earns its place in the genre.
